title: Cornered Resource

content.statement: Sometimes the whole moat is one asset your rivals simply cannot get their hands on.
content.explainer: A cornered resource, in Hamilton Helmer's 7 Powers, is preferential access to a coveted asset, a patent, a location, a mineral deposit, a signature talent, on terms rivals can't match. Its value is the product of how badly others want it and how completely you've locked it up.
questions: Do you hold any asset that rivals genuinely want but cannot obtain at any reasonable price?
Is your exclusive access protected by a durable mechanism, or just by luck that could run out?
If a competitor tried to replicate your edge, exactly which door would be locked to them?
sop: Inventory the assets you control: talent, IP, locations, contracts, data, relationships, supply.
Rate how desirable each asset is to competitors who lack it (see calculation, page 6).
Rate how exclusively you've secured it, how hard it is for rivals to obtain (see calculation, page 6).
Reinforce the strongest cornered resource with legal, contractual, or relational locks.
Avoid betting the business on any asset whose exclusivity is temporary or easily bypassed.

definitions.def: Preferential access to a coveted asset that competitors cannot obtain on equal terms, giving you an advantage they can't replicate.
The degree to which you, and no one else, can access or use a given asset.

content.equation: Cornered Resource Value = Asset Desirability × Exclusivity
content.equation_example: Asset Desirability 9 × Exclusivity 8 = 72. High on both means a durable, hard-to-attack advantage; a coveted asset with weak exclusivity (9 × 2 = 18) is a prize rivals will soon share.
content.closing_statement: A real advantage is something others want and cannot simply go out and buy.
content.question: Do you hold any asset that rivals genuinely want but cannot obtain at any reasonable price?

caption:
Principle Nº 347: Cornered Resource

Sometimes the whole moat is one asset your rivals simply cannot get their hands on.

Ask yourself: Do you hold any asset that rivals genuinely want but cannot obtain at any reasonable price?
